dm_verity_root_hash_evidence: kernel-cmdline root_hash, roothash, usrhash, or dm-mod.create verity table
device-mapper root device sysfs name/uuid when exposed by the booted image

required_kernel_options.disabled_or_absent_rationale: Attack-surface trim asserted against the kernel the build actually ships (stock Talos v1.13.0, no custom kernel). Operators do not use Bluetooth or wireless drivers (wired network only) and do not mount legacy filesystems, so those subsystems are absent. NOTE: the Whitepaper v5.0 section 21.2 trim of the AF_ALG/algif crypto-socket family (CONFIG_CRYPTO_USER_API*) and the VM/KVM stack (CONFIG_VIRTUALIZATION, CONFIG_KVM) is DEFERRED — those options are compiled into the stock Talos kernel and cannot be asserted absent until a custom hardened kernel (siderolabs/kernel rebuild with those set to =n, wired into build-metal.sh) is produced; tracked as a follow-up. The in-process Rust crypto (ML-DSA-65, ML-KEM, BLAKE3, SLH-DSA) never uses the kernel crypto API regardless. Each token below must be absent or =n in /proc/config.gz.
